title: Pokemon Detail Lookup, Derived Views, Units, and Provenance · Guided Systems Studio
summary: A regional field-research team incrementally builds human-readable validated Pokemon detail view from original fixed PokéAPI fixtures while retaining prior Go, HTTP, Git, quality-gate, accessibility, security, testing, cancellation, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of human-readable validated Pokemon detail view while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Accept numeric ID or constrained canonical name, retain requested and resolved identity, and reject ambiguous or hostile input.
Model stable identity, species, types, abilities, stats, dimensions, and optional fields separately from the transport DTO.
Convert height and weight only under documented API units, label original and displayed units, and test rounding boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Pokedex Go Pokemon Lookup…
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 8308 during an accessible regional field-guide session. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate Pokemon detail fields, unit conversions, ordered presentation, and provenance; the release claim must identify the exact source and artifact revision; then change one catch, owned record, encounter relation, or history state and prove the prior invariant. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?
